info.title: Weatherstack API
info.description: The Weatherstack API was built to deliver accurate weather data for any application and use case, from real-time and historical weather information along with advances weather stats like Marine data all the way to 14-day weather forecasts, supporting all major programming languages. Our straightforward API design will make it easy to use the API.

get.description: To query the Weatherstack API for real-time weather data in a location of your choice, simply attach your preferred location to the API's `current` endpoint as seen in the example request below. Depending on your subscription, you can also make a bulk location request by passing multiple semicolon-separated locations to the API URL.

parameters.name: access_key
parameters.description: Your Weatherstack API access key. Find it in your Weatherstack dashboard.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description:
Free-text location identifier. Examples: `New York`, `London, UK`, `51.5074,-0.1278`,
`fetch:ip` (auto-detect by requester's IP). For bulk queries pass semicolon-separated
locations (Professional and higher plans).

parameters.name: units
parameters.description: Units system for returned values. `m` = Metric (default), `s` = Scientific, `f` = Fahrenheit.

parameters.name: language
parameters.description: Optional ISO 2-letter language code to localize text fields (where supported).

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: JSONP callback function name. When present, the API response is wrapped for JSONP.

200.description:
Current Weather Data.

The successful API request from the example above now returns real-time weather data for the city of New York, including detailed information about temperature, humidity, wind, clouds, pressure, the current time, a series of location identifiers, and more.

schema.description: Response schema for current weather requests.

type.description: Type of location lookup performed (e.g., City, LatLon, IP, Zipcode).

query.example: New York, United States of America

language.description: ISO 2-letter language code used for the response.

unit.description: Units system for returned values (m, s, or f).

request.description: Information about the request and query parameters.

name.description: Name of the location (e.g., city or place).

country.description: Country where the location is situated.

region.description: Region, state, or province of the location.

timezone_id.description: Time zone identifier for the location.

localtime.description: Local date and time at the location.

localtime_epoch.description: Local time as Unix epoch seconds.

utc_offset.description: Offset from UTC in hours for the location.

location.description: Location details for the current weather.

weather_code.description: Weather condition code.

weather_icons.description: Array of icon URLs representing the weather.

weather_descriptions.description: Array of text descriptions of the weather.

sunrise.description: Time of sunrise at the location.

sunset.description: Time of sunset at the location.

moonrise.description: Time of moonrise at the location.

moonset.description: Time of moonset at the location.

wind_dir.description: Wind direction as a compass point.

feelslike.description: Apparent temperature (feels like).

current.description: Current weather conditions at the location.

info.example: You have not supplied an API access_key.

get.description: The Weatherstack API is capable of returning weather forecast data for up to 14 days into the future. To get weather forecasts, simply use the API's Weather Forecast endpoint and define your preferred number of forecast days using the `forecast_days` parameter.

parameters.name: access_key
parameters.description: Your Weatherstack API access key. Find it in your Weatherstack dashboard.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description:
Free-text location identifier. Examples: `New York`, `London, UK`, `51.5074,-0.1278`,
`fetch:ip` (auto-detect by requester's IP). For bulk queries pass semicolon-separated
locations (Professional and higher plans).

parameters.name: forecast_days
parameters.description: Number of days of forecast to return (depends on subscription; up to 14 days).

parameters.name: hourly
parameters.description: Set to `1` to include hourly breakdown, or `0` (default) to omit hourly data.

parameters.name: interval
parameters.description:
When `hourly=1`, defines the spacing of hourly entries: 1 (hourly), 3 (default), 6, 12,
or 24 (day average).

parameters.name: units
parameters.description: Units system for returned values. `m` = Metric (default), `s` = Scientific, `f` = Fahrenheit.

parameters.name: language
parameters.description: Optional ISO 2-letter language code to localize text fields (where supported).

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: JSONP callback function name. When present, the API response is wrapped for JSONP.

schema.description: Response schema for weather forecast requests.

type.description: Type of location lookup performed (e.g., City, LatLon, IP, Zipcode).

query.example: New York, United States of America

language.description: ISO 2-letter language code used for the response.

unit.description: Units system for returned values (m, s, or f).

request.description: Information about the request and query parameters.

utc_offset.description: Offset from UTC in hours for the location.

weather_code.description: Weather condition code.

weather_icons.description: Array of icon URLs representing the weather.

weather_descriptions.description: Array of text descriptions of the weather.

sunrise.description: Time of sunrise at the location.

sunset.description: Time of sunset at the location.

moonrise.description: Time of moonrise at the location.

moonset.description: Time of moonset at the location.

wind_dir.description: Wind direction as a compass point.

feelslike.description: Apparent temperature (feels like).

current.description: Current weather conditions at the location.

forecast.description: Forecast data for one or more days.

date_epoch.description: Forecast date as Unix epoch seconds.

sunrise.description: Time of sunrise at the location.

sunset.description: Time of sunset at the location.

moonrise.description: Time of moonrise at the location.

moonset.description: Time of moonset at the location.

astro.description: Astronomical data for the forecast day.

sunhour.description: Total hours of sunshine for the day.

air_quality.description: Air quality measurements for the day.

hourly.description: Hourly weather forecast entries for the day.

weather_code.description: Weather condition code for the hour.

weather_icons.description: Array of icon URLs representing the weather.

weather_descriptions.description: Array of text descriptions of the weather.

heatindex.description: Heat index value for the hour.

dewpoint.description: Dew point temperature for the hour.

windchill.description: Wind chill temperature for the hour.

windgust.description: Wind gust speed for the hour.

feelslike.description: Apparent temperature (feels like) for the hour.

uv_index.description: UV index value for the hour.

air_quality.description: Air quality measurements for the hour.

info.example: You have not supplied an API access_key.

get.description: To look up historical weather data all the way back to 2015, simply pass one date of your choice (later than July 2008) or multiple semicolon-separated dates to the Weatherstack API's Historical Weather endpoint using the  `historical_date` parameter.

parameters.name: access_key
parameters.description: Your Weatherstack API access key. Find it in your Weatherstack dashboard.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description:
Free-text location identifier. Examples: `New York`, `London, UK`, `51.5074,-0.1278`,
`fetch:ip` (auto-detect by requester's IP). For bulk queries pass semicolon-separated
locations (Professional and higher plans).

parameters.name: historical_date
parameters.description:
Single historical date (YYYY-MM-DD) or semicolon-separated list of dates. Used to
request specific historical days (availability depends on subscription and archives).

parameters.name: historical_date_start
parameters.description: Start date for a historical time-series (required for time-series requests).

parameters.name: historical_date_end
parameters.description: End date for a historical time-series request (maximum timeframe: 60 days).

parameters.name: hourly
parameters.description: Set to `1` to include hourly breakdown, or `0` (default) to omit hourly data.

parameters.name: interval
parameters.description:
When `hourly=1`, defines the spacing of hourly entries: 1 (hourly), 3 (default), 6, 12,
or 24 (day average).

parameters.name: units
parameters.description: Units system for returned values. `m` = Metric (default), `s` = Scientific, `f` = Fahrenheit.

parameters.name: language
parameters.description: Optional ISO 2-letter language code to localize text fields (where supported).

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: JSONP callback function name. When present, the API response is wrapped for JSONP.

200.description:
Historical Weather Data.

In addition to the requested historical weather data, a successful historical weather API call will also return the current weather in the location used for the request, as well as information about the API request and location.

**Note:** *The response objects `request`, `location` and `current` will not be explained below as they are already mentioned in the Current Weather Endpoint section above.*

type.description: Type of location lookup performed (e.g., City, LatLon, IP, Zipcode).

localtime_epoch.description: Local time as Unix epoch seconds.

utc_offset.description: Offset from UTC in hours for the location.

location.description: Location details for the historical data.

weather_code.description: Weather condition code.

weather_icons.description: Array of icon URLs representing the weather.

weather_descriptions.description: Array of text descriptions of the weather.

sunrise.description: Time of sunrise at the location.

sunset.description: Time of sunset at the location.

moonrise.description: Time of moonrise at the location.

moonset.description: Time of moonset at the location.

wind_dir.description: Wind direction as a compass point.

feelslike.description: Apparent temperature (feels like).

current.description: Current weather conditions at the location.

historical.description: Historical weather data for one or more days.

date_epoch.description: Historical date as Unix epoch seconds.

sunhour.description: Total hours of sunshine for the day.

hourly.description: Hourly weather data entries for the day.

weather_code.description: Weather condition code for the hour.

weather_icons.description: Array of icon URLs representing the weather.

weather_descriptions.description: Array of text descriptions of the weather.

heatindex.description: Heat index value for the hour.

dewpoint.description: Dew point temperature for the hour.

windchill.description: Wind chill temperature for the hour.

windgust.description: Wind gust speed for the hour.

feelslike.description: Apparent temperature (feels like) for the hour.

uv_index.description: UV index value for the hour.

air_quality.description: Air quality measurements for the hour.

info.example: You have not supplied an API access_key.

get.description: The Marine Weather API endpoint will allow to access today's live marine/sailing weather forecast for a given `longitude` and `latitude` , as well as up to 7 days of forecast.

parameters.name: access_key
parameters.description: Your Weatherstack API access key. Find it in your Weatherstack dashboard.

parameters.name: latitude
parameters.description: Latitude coordinate (decimal degrees) for marine endpoints.

parameters.name: longitude
parameters.description: Longitude coordinate (decimal degrees) for marine endpoints.

parameters.name: tide
parameters.description: Set `tide=yes` to include tidal information (where available). Defaults to `no`.

parameters.name: hourly
parameters.description: Set to `1` to include hourly breakdown, or `0` (default) to omit hourly data.

parameters.name: interval
parameters.description:
When `hourly=1`, defines the spacing of hourly entries: 1 (hourly), 3 (default), 6, 12,
or 24 (day average).

parameters.name: units
parameters.description: Units system for returned values. `m` = Metric (default), `s` = Scientific, `f` = Fahrenheit.

parameters.name: language
parameters.description: Optional ISO 2-letter language code to localize text fields (where supported).

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: JSONP callback function name. When present, the API response is wrapped for JSONP.

schema.description: Response schema for live marine and sailing weather requests.

type.description: Type of location lookup performed (e.g., City, LatLon, IP, Zipcode).

query.example: New York, United States of America

language.description: ISO 2-letter language code used for the response.

unit.description: Units system for returned values (m, s, or f).

request.description: Information about the request and query parameters.

forecast.description: Marine weather forecast data for one or more days.

items.description: Represents a single day of marine weather forecast data.

date_epoch.description: Forecast date as Unix epoch seconds.

sunrise.description: Time of sunrise at the location.

sunset.description: Time of sunset at the location.

moonrise.description: Time of moonrise at the location.

moonset.description: Time of moonset at the location.

astro.description: Astronomical data for the forecast day.

mintemp.description: Minimum air temperature for the day.

maxtemp.description: Maximum air temperature for the day.

avgtemp.description: Average air temperature for the day.

tides.description: Tidal events for the forecast day.

tideHeight_mt.description: Height of the tide in meters.

tideDateTime.description: Date and time of the tide event.

tide_type.description: Type of tide event (HIGH or LOW).

hourly.description: Hourly marine weather forecast entries for the day.

temperature.description: Air temperature at the given hour.

wind_speed.description: Wind speed at the given hour.

wind_dir.description: Wind direction as a compass point.

weather_code.description: Weather condition code for the hour.

weather_icons.description: Array of icon URLs representing the weather.

weather_descriptions.description: Array of text descriptions of the weather.

heatindex.description: Heat index value for the hour.

dewpoint.description: Dew point temperature for the hour.

windchill.description: Wind chill temperature for the hour.

windgust.description: Wind gust speed for the hour.

feelslike.description: Apparent temperature (feels like) for the hour.

water_temp.description: Sea surface temperature at the given hour.

uv_index.description: UV index value for the hour.

info.example: You have not supplied an API access_key.

get.description:
The Historical Marine Weather API have marine data since 1st Jan, 2015 for a given `latitude` and `longitude`, as well as tide data. This endpoint will return weather elements such as temperature, precipitation (rainfall), weather description, weather icon, wind speed, Tide data, significant wave height, swell height, swell direction and swell period.   
 
**Note:** *Each day and location included in your request counts toward your monthly API request limit. The historical marine time series supports a maximum time frame of 35 days.*

parameters.name: access_key
parameters.description: Your Weatherstack API access key. Find it in your Weatherstack dashboard.

parameters.name: latitude
parameters.description: Latitude coordinate (decimal degrees) for marine endpoints.

parameters.name: longitude
parameters.description: Longitude coordinate (decimal degrees) for marine endpoints.

parameters.name: historical_date_start
parameters.description: Start date for a historical time-series (required for time-series requests).

parameters.name: historical_date_end
parameters.description: End date for a historical time-series request (maximum timeframe: 60 days).

parameters.name: tide
parameters.description: Set `tide=yes` to include tidal information (where available). Defaults to `no`.

parameters.name: hourly
parameters.description: Set to `1` to include hourly breakdown, or `0` (default) to omit hourly data.

parameters.name: interval
parameters.description:
When `hourly=1`, defines the spacing of hourly entries: 1 (hourly), 3 (default), 6, 12,
or 24 (day average).

parameters.name: units
parameters.description: Units system for returned values. `m` = Metric (default), `s` = Scientific, `f` = Fahrenheit.

parameters.name: language
parameters.description: Optional ISO 2-letter language code to localize text fields (where supported).

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: JSONP callback function name. When present, the API response is wrapped for JSONP.

type.description: Type of location lookup performed (e.g., City, LatLon, IP, Zipcode).

language.description: ISO 2-letter language code used for the response.

unit.description: Units system for returned values (m, s, or f).

request.description: Information about the request and query parameters.

historical.description: Historical marine weather data for one or more days.

additionalProperties.description: Historical marine weather data for a single day.

date_epoch.description: Historical date as Unix epoch seconds.

sunrise.description: Time of sunrise at the location.

sunset.description: Time of sunset at the location.

moonrise.description: Time of moonrise at the location.

moonset.description: Time of moonset at the location.

astro.description: Astronomical data for the historical day.

mintemp.description: Minimum air temperature for the day.

maxtemp.description: Maximum air temperature for the day.

avgtemp.description: Average air temperature for the day.

tides.description: Tidal events for the historical day.

tideHeight_mt.description: Height of the tide in meters.

tideDateTime.description: Date and time of the tide event.

tide_type.description: Type of tide event (HIGH or LOW).

hourly.description: Hourly marine weather data entries for the day.

temperature.description: Air temperature at the given hour.

wind_speed.description: Wind speed at the given hour.

wind_dir.description: Wind direction as a compass point.

weather_code.description: Weather condition code for the hour.

weather_icons.description: Array of icon URLs representing the weather.

weather_descriptions.description: Array of text descriptions of the weather.

heatindex.description: Heat index value for the hour.

dewpoint.description: Dew point temperature for the hour.

windchill.description: Wind chill temperature for the hour.

windgust.description: Wind gust speed for the hour.

feelslike.description: Apparent temperature (feels like) for the hour.

water_temp.description: Sea surface temperature at the given hour.

uv_index.description: UV index value for the hour.

info.example: You have not supplied an API access_key.

get.description: The Weatherstack API's Location Lookup/Autocomplete endpoint can be used to pinpoint one or more specific locations and their identifying response objects with the aim of later passing them to a weather data endpoint. In our example below, we are looking for London, United Kingdom.

parameters.name: access_key
parameters.description: Your Weatherstack API access key. Find it in your Weatherstack dashboard.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description:
Free-text location identifier. Examples: `New York`, `London, UK`, `51.5074,-0.1278`,
`fetch:ip` (auto-detect by requester's IP). For bulk queries pass semicolon-separated
locations (Professional and higher plans).

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: JSONP callback function name. When present, the API response is wrapped for JSONP.

schema.description: Response schema for location autocomplete requests.

name.description: Name of the location (e.g., city or place).

country.description: Country where the location is situated.

region.description: Region, state, or province of the location.

timezone_id.description: Time zone identifier for the location.

utc_offset.description: Offset from UTC in hours for the location.

info.example: You have not supplied an API access_key.
